attractions.name: Barre Opera House (Barre City Hall and Opera House)
attractions.description: The Barre Opera House is a premier historic performing arts venue located on the upper floors of the Barre City Hall. Originally built in 1899 to replace a previous structure lost to fire, it has hosted legendary figures like Helen Keller and Theodore Roosevelt and remains renowned for its exceptional acoustics and elegant Beaux Arts design. Wikipedia, Official Site
attractions.address: 6 North Main Street (at Prospect St), Barre, VT 05641, USA
attractions.hours: Box Office: Wednesday - Friday: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM; Saturday: 9:00 AM - 12:00 PM; Sunday - Tuesday: Closed. Doors typically open 60-90 minutes before scheduled performances. Official Site, Vivid Seats
attractions.days_open: Wednesday - Saturday (Box Office); Event-based for performances.
attractions.admission: Ticket prices generally range from $20 to $94 depending on the event and seating section. Specific membership tiers are available: Student ($30), Individual ($50), Household ($75), Supporting ($150), Patron ($300), Benefactor ($500), Angel ($1,000), and Saint ($2,500+). Rush tickets are occasionally offered for $20 day-of. Barre Opera House, Vivid Seats, Event Tickets Center
highlights: 'Acoustically pure theater seating up to 650 patrons with excellent sightlines from both orchestra and balcony. [Tripadvisor', 'Historic Beaux Arts architecture featuring a three-story recessed arch facade and a wrought iron balcony. Wikipedia', 'Home to the Vermont Symphony Orchestra, Vermont Philharmonic, and various local dance and theater companies. Official Site', 'Features a beautifully reproduced stage curtain depicting the Grand Canal in Venice. The Montpelier Bridge', 'Historic outer balcony where presidential candidates William Howard Taft and Theodore Roosevelt once spoke. Wikipedia']
attractions.tips: Arrive 30-60 minutes early to secure parking in the downtown area. Balcony seating offers historic charm with cast iron seats but may have less legroom than the orchestra section. Refreshments and restrooms are located on the second level; the lobby can get crowded during intermissions. Tripadvisor, TicketSmarter
attractions.nearby: Hope Cemetery (famous for granite sculptures), Rock of Ages Granite Quarry, Studio Place Arts, Aldrich Public Library, and the Vermont Granite Museum. Seven Days, Wanderlog

attractions.name: Bear Swamp Orchard and Cidery (Bear Swamp Orchard - Distillery and Cidery)
attractions.description: Bear Swamp Orchard and Cidery is a small, family-run, certified organic farm and distillery located at the top of the Berkshires. They specialize in traditional, low-intervention hard ciders and apple brandies made from over 50 varieties of sustainably grown heirloom and cider apples. The Boston Globe, The Berkshire Edge
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: Closed; Thursday: Closed; Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (PYO only); Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Tasting Room 1:00 PM – 5:00 PM); Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Tasting Room 1:00 PM – 5:00 PM). Hours are seasonal (approx. Sept-Nov). Visit Massachusetts, Cider Chat
attractions.admission: Free admission; PYO apples and tastings priced by purchase.
highlights: 'Certified Organic orchard growing over 50 varieties of heirloom and cider apples. [The Boston Globe', 'On-site distillery producing organic apple brandy and artisanal liqueurs. Visit Massachusetts', 'Traditional hard cider production using wild yeast and no added sulfites. The Berkshire Edge', 'Scenic hilltop views reaching as far as Vermont and New Hampshire. Outdooractive', 'Offers pick-your-own (PYO) apples, sweet cider, and farm-made jams and jellies. Yelp', 'Borders the Bear Swamp Reservation, providing access to miles of hiking trails. The Trustees of Reservations']
attractions.tips: Visit on weekends in October for the full selection of heirloom apple varieties and wood-fired baked goods. Try the unique "Cyser" (cider fermented with honey) and the award-winning apple brandy. Check their Facebook page or call ahead for updated picking conditions as availability varies. Yelp, The Berkshire Edge

attractions.name: Bohemian Bakery (Bohemian Bakery)
attractions.description: Bohemian Bakery is a European-style bakery and cafe in downtown Montpelier that specializes in artisan pastries, house-roasted coffee, and handmade focaccia sandwiches (Bohemian Bakery, Tripadvisor). The bakery is known for its "All the butter, All the sugar, All the caffeine" mantra and offers a bright, welcoming atmosphere with Parisian-level croissants and tarts (The Montpelier Bridge, Seven Days).
attractions.address: 83 Main Street, Montpelier, Vermont 05602-2931 (Bohemian Bakery, Tripadvisor)
attractions.hours: Wednesday: 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM, Thursday: 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM, Friday: 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM, Saturday: 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM, Sunday: 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM, Monday: Closed, Tuesday: Closed (Bohemian Bakery, Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor)
highlights: 'Award-winning "Paris-level" almond and butter croissants ([Seven Days)', 'Fresh baguettes available hot from the oven around noon, Thursday through Sunday (Bohemian Bakery)', 'House-roasted, rich full-bodied coffee and espresso drinks (Tripadvisor)', 'Daily savory pastries including tomato mozzarella and egg with chives (Bohemian Bakery)', 'Variety of gluten-free options like hazelnut buckwheat cake (Bohemian Bakery)', 'Laptop-free and Wi-Fi-free cafe environment for a social atmosphere (Yelp)']
attractions.tips: Arrive early as popular items like croissants sell out quickly; note that the shop is laptop-free and typically responds to inquiries via email only (Yelp, Bohemian Bakery). For the freshest bread, visit around noon Thursday through Sunday for hot baguettes (Bohemian Bakery).

attractions.name: Bragg Farm Sugar House & Gift Shop
attractions.description: Bragg Farm Sugar House & Gift Shop is an eighth-generation family-operated farm known for its traditional maple syrup production and award-winning maple creemees. Visitors can enjoy free guided tours of the sugarhouse, sample various grades of syrup, and explore a large gift shop filled with Vermont crafts and specialty foods. The farm also features a friendly petting zoo, a children's play area, and a scenic walking trail through the maple woods.
attractions.address: 1005 VT Route 14 North, East Montpelier, VT 05651
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 8:30 AM – 5:00 PM (Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year's Day) Bragg Farm
attractions.days_open: Open 7 days a week Bragg Farm
attractions.admission: Free (No admission required for tours and tastings) Bragg Farm
highlights: 'Eighth-generation family-operated sugarhouse producing traditional Vermont maple syrup [Bragg Farm', 'Free guided tours and tastings featuring a wood-fired evaporator and antique sugaring tools Bragg Farm', 'Award-winning maple ice cream parlor serving "World\'s Best" maple creemees, shakes, and sundaes Bragg Farm', 'Large gift shop offering Vermont crafts, cheeses, specialty foods, and a full range of maple products Bragg Farm', "Family-friendly farm experience with a petting zoo, children's play area, and a scenic maple trail Bragg Farm", 'Educational 15-minute video and exhibits on the history and process of maple sugaring Bragg Farm']
attractions.tips: Visit during the spring sugaring season (late winter/early spring) to see the wood-fired evaporator in action. Be sure to try a maple creemee, which is a local favorite, and take advantage of the free tastings to find your preferred grade of syrup. The Coburn Covered Bridge is just around the corner and makes for a great quick stop. Bragg Farm

The store showcases the cooperative's signature award-winning cheeses alongside a curated selection of Vermont artisanal products, including microbrews, cider, and specialty foods. Cabot Creamery, Christine Burdick Design

attractions.name: Camel's Hump State Park
attractions.description: Camel's Hump State Park is an iconic, undeveloped wilderness area centered around Vermont's third-highest peak (4,083 ft). Known for its distinctive profile featured on the Vermont state quarter, the park spans over 21,000 acres and protects a unique alpine tundra environment. It is a premier destination for hiking, offering challenging trails and 360-degree views of the Green Mountains and Lake Champlain.
attractions.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours, Tuesday: Open 24 hours, Wednesday: Open 24 hours, Thursday: Open 24 hours, Friday: Open 24 hours, Saturday: Open 24 hours, Sunday: Open 24 hours (Note: Trails above 2,500 feet closed during Mud Season)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ["Vermont's third-highest peak at 4,083 feet.", 'Features a distinctive profile depicted on the Vermont state quarter.', 'Largest Natural Area in Vermont, covering over 21,000 acres.', 'Iconic hiking trails including the Long Trail, Burrows Trail, and Monroe Trail.', 'Rare alpine tundra vegetation on the summit summit.', 'Superb 360-degree views of the Green Mountains and Lake Champlain.']
attractions.tips: Arrive early on weekends as parking lots fill quickly; carpooling is recommended. Stay off trails during "mud season" (typically mid-April to Memorial Day) to prevent erosion. Keep dogs leashed, especially above the treeline, to protect fragile alpine plants. Carry a map and be prepared for rapid weather changes at the summit.
attractions.nearby: Little River State Park, Waterbury Reservoir, Mount Mansfield, Green Mountain Club Visitor Center

attractions.name: Capital City Farmers Market
attractions.description: The Capital City Farmers Market is a vibrant year-round marketplace that has been bringing local producers and customers together in Montpelier since 1977. It offers a wide variety of farm-fresh produce, prepared ethnic foods, and high-quality artisan crafts from over 50 local vendors. The market serves as a central community gathering spot, often featuring live music and a family-friendly atmosphere.
attractions.hours: Monday-Friday: Closed; Saturday: 9:00 AM – 1:00 PM (Summer, May-Oct) / 10:00 AM – 1:00 PM (Winter, Dec-Apr, 1st & 3rd Sats); Sunday: Closed
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['Wide selection of fresh, locally grown organic produce and heirloom vegetables', 'Diverse prepared food options including Taiwanese street food, Filipino cuisine, and tamales', 'Award-winning local cheeses, grass-fed meats, and fresh seafood from Vermont producers', 'Handcrafted artisan goods such as pottery, woodworking, jewelry, and letterpress cards', 'Live musical performances and a lively social atmosphere during the summer season', 'Year-round availability with specific indoor winter and outdoor summer schedules']
attractions.tips: Arrive early at 9:00 AM for the best selection of seasonal items. Most vendors accept cash, and some also take credit cards or EBT. Pets are welcome but must be kept on a short leash, and bicycles should be walked within the market area. Check the official website for specific winter dates and the annual Thanksgiving Holiday Market location.
attractions.nearby: Vermont State House, Vermont Historical Society Museum, Hubbard Park, Downtown Montpelier shops

attractions.name: Central Vermont Marketplace (formerly Berlin Mall)
attractions.description: Central Vermont Marketplace is a premier regional shopping destination in the Montpelier area, recently rebranded from its former name, Berlin Mall. The center serves as a mixed-use community hub, featuring a variety of national retailers, local shops, and professional services, as well as a senior living facility on-site (Heidenberg Properties).
attractions.hours: Monday-Saturday: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM; Sunday: 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Note: Individual store hours such as Walmart and Planet Fitness may extend beyond these times) (Five Below, Hobby Lobby)
attractions.days_open: 7 Days a Week (Individual store hours vary) (Central Vermont Marketplace)
attractions.admission: Free (Central Vermont Marketplace)
highlights: "Home to major national anchors including Walmart, Kohl's, and Hobby Lobby ([Central Vermont Marketplace)", 'Features an enhanced internal common area with a new art wall designed by local artist Hannah Morris (Central Vermont Marketplace)', 'Part of a mixed-use development that includes the 98-unit Chestnut Place senior living facility (Shopping Center Business)', 'Strategically located near I-89, serving the communities of Montpelier and Barre (Heidenberg Properties)', 'Diverse tenant mix including Planet Fitness, Five Below, and a U.S. Post Office branch (Central Vermont Marketplace)']
attractions.tips: Visitors should check the hours for specific major retailers like Walmart or Hobby Lobby, as they often differ from the mall's general common area hours. Be sure to explore the internal hallways to see the local art installations that are part of the mall's recent rebranding (Central Vermont Marketplace).

attractions.name: Church Street Marketplace
attractions.description: The Church Street Marketplace is an award-winning, four-block outdoor pedestrian mall in the heart of downtown Burlington. It serves as a vibrant community hub featuring over 100 local and national shops, a diverse range of restaurants with outdoor dining, and historic Victorian and Art Deco architecture. The marketplace is renowned for its lively atmosphere, hosting year-round festivals, street performers, and seasonal events that attract over 1.5 million visitors annually.
attractions.address: 131 Church St (extending four blocks between Main and Pearl Streets)
attractions.hours: The pedestrian mall is accessible 24/7. Individual shop and restaurant hours typically vary: Monday-Saturday: 10:00 AM - 7:00 PM; Sunday: 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM. (Many restaurants remain open later for dinner and nightlife).
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['Historic National Register Historic District with stunning 19th-century architecture.', 'Over 100 unique shops and boutiques alongside major national retailers.', 'More than 30 diverse dining options ranging from casual pubs to fine dining.', 'Year-round community festivals, including the Discover Jazz Festival and Festival of Fools.', 'Lively street entertainment featuring local musicians, magicians, and artists.', 'Iconic holiday displays including a giant Christmas tree and thousands of twinkly lights.']
attractions.tips: Take advantage of 2 hours of free parking daily at the Downtown Garage (formerly College & Lakeview) with ParkMobile check-in. Parking is free on Sundays and city holidays at all city-owned facilities. The marketplace is highly dog-friendly and perfect for people-watching from outdoor cafes during the warmer months.
attractions.nearby: Waterfront Park & Lake Champlain, ECHO Leahy Center for Lake Champlain, Flynn Center for the Performing Arts, Burlington Bike Path, South End Arts District.

attractions.name: City of Barre (City of Barre Official)
attractions.description: Known as the "Granite Capital of the World," the City of Barre is a historic center of Vermont's granite industry featuring a walkable downtown set in stone. The area is celebrated for its impressive granite architecture, world-renowned cemeteries, and vibrant arts scene, making it a unique cultural and historical destination (Vermont Tourism).
attractions.address: 6 North Main Street, Barre, VT 05641 (City of Barre Official)
attractions.hours: City Hall: Monday - Friday, 7:30 AM - 4:30 PM (Vermont Attorney's Title); Museums (e.g., Granite Museum): Tuesday - Saturday, 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM (May - Oct) (Vermont Granite Museum)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday for official city business; Tuesday - Saturday for major museums (seasonal) (Vermont Granite Museum, Vermont Attorney's Title)
attractions.admission: Free for general downtown exploration and Hope Cemetery; Vermont Granite Museum: $15 Adults, $12 Seniors/Veterans, $8 Children (Vermont Granite Museum); Vermont History Center: $7 Adults, $5 Children/Seniors (Tripadvisor)
highlights: 'Vermont Granite Museum: Housed in a turn-of-the-century processing plant ([Vermont Tourism)', 'Hope Cemetery: Renowned for its intricate and artistic granite gravestones (Tripadvisor)', 'Barre Opera House: An 1899 cultural cornerstone and performance venue (Vermont Tourism)', '"Unzipping the Earth": North America\'s largest granite zipper sculpture (Seven Days)', 'Studio Place Arts: A community visual arts center with three floors of galleries (Studio Place Arts)']
attractions.tips: Visit between May and October for the best access to seasonal museum tours and the Rock of Ages quarry. Be sure to explore the Art Stroll for granite sculptures hidden throughout downtown (Vermont Tourism, Seven Days).
attractions.nearby: Montpelier (State Capital), Rock of Ages Quarry, Millstone Trails, Groton State Forest (Vermont Tourism)

attractions.name: Coburn Covered Bridge (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: Built in 1851 by Larned Coburn, this historic queenpost truss bridge is the town's only surviving 19th-century covered bridge. It spans the Winooski River in East Montpelier and has been listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 1974. (Wikipedia, Vermont Covered Bridge Society)
attractions.hours: Monday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Tuesday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Wednesday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Thursday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Friday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Saturday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Sunday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM (Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free (Vermont Tourism)
attractions.phone: (802) 223-3313 (East Montpelier Town Office) (Town of East Montpelier)
highlights: 'The only surviving 19th-century covered bridge in the town of East Montpelier ([Wikipedia)', 'A rare example of a queenpost truss structure with embedded iron rods (Wikipedia)', 'Spans the scenic Winooski River, providing picturesque views for photography (Old Stagecoach Inn)', 'Listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 1974 (Wikipedia)', 'Originally built and gifted to the town by local resident Larned Coburn (Vermont Covered Bridge Society)']
attractions.tips: Use GPS for navigation as it is not visible from the main highway. Parking is available safely along the roadside near the bridge. Visit during the fall for vibrant foliage or early morning for tranquil, crowd-free photography sessions. (Evendo, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Green Mount Cemetery (City of Montpelier)
attractions.description: Green Mount Cemetery is a 35-acre rural cemetery established in 1854, designed in the 19th-century "garden cemetery" style to embrace the untamed features of the landscape (City of Montpelier). It is renowned for its terraced lots, winding roads, and a wide array of sculptured memorials created by local artisans from Vermont's granite and marble industries (Wikipedia)).
attractions.hours: Grounds: Daily, sunrise to sunset; Office: Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM (U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs)
attractions.admission: Free (TripAdvisor)
highlights: 'Gothic Revival Chapel-Vault entrance (1905) ([City of Montpelier)', '"Black Agnes" haunted bronze statue (Wikipedia))', "U.S. Soldiers' Lot from the Civil War (U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs)", '"Stairway to Nowhere" hand-carved into the granite hillside (TripAdvisor)', 'Memorial to "Little Margaret" Pitkin (TripAdvisor)', 'Scenic views of Montpelier and the Winooski River (City of Montpelier)']
attractions.tips: Visitors should wear comfortable walking shoes to navigate the hilly terrain. Because the roads are narrow and bumpy, it is recommended to park near the entrance and explore the grounds on foot (TripAdvisor).
attractions.nearby: Hubbard Park, Vermont State House, Vermont Historical Society Museum, Winooski River (TripAdvisor, Vermont Tourism)

attractions.name: Hope Cemetery (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: Established in 1895, Hope Cemetery is a world-renowned outdoor museum showcasing the exceptional artistry of Barre's granite stonecutters. The 65-acre grounds feature over 10,000 intricately carved monuments, many of which were created by the very artisans who are now interred there, serving as a testament to the city's heritage as the "Granite Capital of the World." (Vermonter, Atlas Obscura)
attractions.hours: 7:00 AM – Dusk (Daily) (TeamThomasTravels)
attractions.admission: Free (Airial Travel)
highlights: 'Life-sized "Racing Car" monument ([Vermonter)', '"Soccer Ball" monument on a pedestal (Vermonter)', '"Smoking Chair" representation of a man\'s favorite pastime (Vermonter)', '"Dying Man" sculpture depicting a figure on a deathbed (Vermonter)', 'The unfinished masterpiece of sculptor Elia Corti (Vermonter)', 'Elaborate family mausoleums with classical architectural elements (Vermonter)']
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes to explore the extensive 65-acre grounds, or take a slow drive through the paved paths if time is limited. Photography is encouraged, but visitors should remain respectful of the site's primary function as a resting place and avoid disturbing any active services. (Tripadvisor, Roadside America)
attractions.nearby: Rock of Ages Granite Quarry, Vermont Granite Museum, Barre Opera House, Bragg Farm Sugar House, Vermont State House (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Hubbard Park and Tower
attractions.description: Hubbard Park is a nearly 200-acre natural area in the heart of Montpelier featuring over seven miles of trails for hiking and skiing. Its crown jewel is a 54-foot historic stone observation tower, completed in 1930, which offers sweeping views of the Vermont State House dome and the surrounding mountain ranges (City of Montpelier, Wikipedia)).
attractions.hours: Dawn to Dusk, or 6:00 AM - 9:00 PM, whichever is longer (City of Montpelier)
attractions.admission: Free (City of Montpelier)
highlights: '54-foot historic stone observation tower with panoramic views ([Wikipedia))', 'Over 7 miles of multi-use hiking and cross-country skiing trails (City of Montpelier)', "Seven historic 'crumbling' fireplaces located in a secluded forest glen (City of Montpelier)", 'Two sheltered picnic pavilions, including the universally accessible New Shelter (City of Montpelier)', 'A popular sledding hill for winter recreation (Vermont.com)', 'Stone Tower Trail, a 1-mile accessible path from the New Shelter (Trail Finder)']
attractions.tips: For the easiest walk to the tower, park at the intersection of Parkside Drive and Hubbard Park Drive for a 10-minute stroll (Tripadvisor). The park follows a strict 'carry-in, carry-out' trash policy and features designated off-leash zones for dogs (City of Montpelier).
attractions.nearby: Vermont State House, North Branch Nature Center, T.W. Wood Gallery & Arts Center, Downtown Montpelier (Evendo)

attractions.name: Kellogg-Hubbard Library
attractions.description: The Kellogg-Hubbard Library is a non-profit public library and a monumental example of Italian Renaissance style architecture represented in Vermont granite. Founded in 1894 and opened in 1896, it serves as a dynamic community hub for several Central Vermont towns. The library offers free access to nearly 70,000 items, hosts the annual PoemCity celebration, and features a dedicated children's library and teen center (Kellogg-Hubbard Library, East Montpelier Request Docs).
attractions.hours: Monday: 10 am–8 pm; Tuesday: 10 am–5:30 pm; Wednesday: 10 am–8 pm (late open at 12 pm on first Wednesday of the month); Thursday: 10 am–5:30 pm; Friday: 10 am–5:30 pm; Saturday: 10 am–2 pm; Sunday: Closed (Kellogg-Hubbard Library)
attractions.admission: Free (Airial Travel, Kellogg-Hubbard Library)
highlights: 'Stunning Italian Renaissance architecture built with Vermont granite ([Kellogg-Hubbard Library)', 'Annual PoemCity celebration in April, featuring poetry in downtown windows (Airial Travel)', 'StoryWalk® Project, an outdoor path where visitors read a story page by page (Airial Travel)', 'The Basement Teen Center, providing a dedicated space for youth activities and programs (Kellogg-Hubbard Library)', 'Rich collection of historical books focused on Vermont and local heritage (Airial Travel)', 'Classic 1896 interior features, including original woodwork and a Pantheon frieze (Maplecroft Bed & Breakfast)']
attractions.tips: Downtown parking is mostly limited to 2 hours; however, it is free after 5 PM and on weekends. The nonfiction room and upstairs adult fiction room are designated quiet zones for study. Visitors should visit in April to see the PoemCity displays across the city (Airial Travel, Kellogg-Hubbard Library).
attractions.nearby: Vermont State House, Hubbard Park, Vermont Historical Society Museum, Downtown Montpelier (Montpelier Alive)

attractions.name: Lake Champlain (Burlington Waterfront Park)
attractions.description: Lake Champlain is a majestic 120-mile-long freshwater lake nestled between Vermont's Green Mountains and New York's Adirondacks. The Burlington Waterfront Park serves as the city's premier access point, offering sprawling green spaces, scenic boardwalks, and world-class sunset views over the water. It is a central hub for recreation, hosting numerous festivals, boat tours, and direct access to the famous Burlington Bike Path.
attractions.hours: Daily: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM (Park hours); Seasonal facilities and attractions vary
attractions.admission: Free (Park access); separate fees for ECHO Museum and boat tours
highlights: ['Panoramic views of the Adirondack Mountains across the water', 'Access to the 14-mile Burlington Bike Path for cycling and walking', 'ECHO Leahy Center for Lake Champlain, a world-class science and nature museum', 'Narrated scenic cruises and sunset tours on the Spirit of Ethan Allen boat', 'Community Boathouse Marina featuring public docks and lakeside dining', 'Large wooden swings along the promenade for relaxing by the lakefront']
attractions.tips: Arrive early to secure parking at the Pease Lot or Lake Street lots, which use the ParkMobile app for payment. The park is best visited at sunset, and the waterfront swings provide the best vantage point for photos. For a full day trip, combine your visit with a walk to the nearby Church Street Marketplace for shopping and dining.
attractions.nearby: Church Street Marketplace, ECHO Leahy Center for Lake Champlain, Battery Park, Burlington Community Boathouse

attractions.name: Langdon Street Historic District
attractions.description: Langdon Street is a historic and charming side street in downtown Montpelier, originally envisioned in the 1880s as an elegant shopping complex by local banker James Langdon. The street is celebrated for its uniform 19th-century brick architecture, vibrant creative atmosphere, and the historic Langdon Street Bridge, serving as a hub for independent shops and nightlife. The Montpelier Bridge, Montpelier Alive

attractions.name: Lost Nation Theater
attractions.description: Lost Nation Theater is a professional resident theater company located in the historic Montpelier City Hall. It is dedicated to staging stories about the hopeful possibilities of human interaction and transforming people and communities through the power of theater (Lost Nation Theater, Vermont.com).

attractions.name: Main Street Montpelier / Downtown Montpelier
attractions.description: Downtown Montpelier, centered on the intersection of Main and State Streets, is the vibrant heart of the smallest capital city in the United States. It is renowned for its well-preserved 19th-century architecture, a high density of independent boutiques, and a flourishing farm-to-table culinary scene. As the largest urban historic district in Vermont, it serves as the cultural and economic hub for the surrounding region. (Montpelier Alive, Vermont Tourism)
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Tuesday: 7:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Wednesday: 7:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Thursday: 7:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Friday: 7:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Sunday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Visitor Center hours; district is always accessible). (Montpelier Alive)

attractions.name: Montpelier Historic District
attractions.description: The Montpelier Historic District is the largest urban historic district in Vermont, encompassing the commercial and government heart of the state capital. It features over 450 buildings that showcase a diverse array of 19th-century architectural styles, including Greek Revival, Italianate, and Queen Anne. The district is a vibrant center for state government and independent local commerce, focused around the confluence of the Winooski and North Branch rivers. (Wikipedia), Montpelier Alive)
attractions.address: State Street & Main Street, Montpelier, VT 05602
attractions.hours: Public areas: Open 24/7; Visitor Center: Monday–Friday 7:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Saturday–Sunday 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Montpelier Alive)
attractions.admission: Free (Montpelier Alive, Airial Travel)
highlights: ['Vermont State House, a neoclassical masterpiece with an iconic gold dome.', 'Vermont History Museum, located in the historic-looking Pavilion Building.', 'Seven historic bridges that are beautifully illuminated during the winter months.', 'Langdon Street, known for its unique shopping and 19th-century charm.', 'A rich collection of well-preserved 19th-century architecture.', 'Vibrant downtown area filled with independently owned boutiques and eateries.']
attractions.tips: Start your visit at the local Visitor Center to pick up a free walking tour map and enjoy a complimentary cup of coffee. Visit on a Thursday evening during the summer to experience the 'Montpelier Ramble,' where many shops stay open late with live music. (Montpelier Alive, Montpelier Alive Resources)
attractions.nearby: Hubbard Park, North Branch Nature Center, Winooski River, Kellogg-Hubbard Library (Montpelier Alive)

attractions.name: Montpelier and Wells River Railroad Heritage (Rail Trail)
attractions.description: The Montpelier and Wells River Railroad was a vital 38-mile short-line railroad that operated from 1873 to 1956, famously known as 'The Milk Run' for its service to local villages. Today, its legacy is preserved as a scenic rail trail and a series of historical markers that trace the path of the Winooski and Wells Rivers through central Vermont. Visitors can explore segments of the original railbed, which features historic granite stonework and modern bridges connecting downtown Montpelier to the surrounding countryside. Cross Vermont Trail Association
attractions.address: 29 Main Street, Suite #4 (Cross Vermont Trail Association Office) or Stonecutters Way Trailhead
attractions.hours: Dawn to Dusk (Trail segments); Office hours vary by season.
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ["Scenic path following the Winooski and Wells River valleys along the historic 'Milk Run' route.", "Historical markers on Stonecutters Way detailing the railroad's impact and the 1927 flood.", 'Original 19th-century granite bridge abutments and stonework visible throughout the trail.', 'A 200-foot multi-use bridge over the Winooski River, a key link in the regional trail network.', 'Direct access to the extensive trail systems and state parks within Groton State Forest.', 'Multi-use functionality supporting walking, cycling, and winter snowmobiling in designated sections.']
attractions.tips: Start your journey at the Taylor Street or Stonecutters Way trailheads in Montpelier for easy access to historical markers. Be aware that the trail is a 'patchwork quilt' with some gaps requiring travel on public roads like Route 302. Check the official Cross Vermont Trail website for current trail alerts and construction updates. Sturdy footwear is recommended for unpaved sections of the railbed.
attractions.nearby: Vermont State House, Sarducci’s (housed in a former M&WR baggage building), Groton State Forest, Granite Street Bridge, and the Vermont Historical Society Museum.

attractions.name: Montshire Museum of Science
attractions.description: The Montshire Museum of Science is an award-winning hands-on science museum located on a 100-acre site along the Connecticut River. It features over 150 interactive exhibits indoors and out, focusing on natural and physical sciences, technology, and ecology for visitors of all ages (Montshire Museum of Science) (Wikipedia).

attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed on Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year's Eve)
attractions.admission: Adults: $21; Children (2–17): $18; Members and Children under 2: Free; Two-Day Pass: $31.50 (Adult), $27 (Child); Warm Welcome (EBT/Medicaid/School lunch): $3; Montshire Passbook (12 passes): $190 (Montshire Museum of Science) (Montshire Museum of Science)
highlights: 'Science Park: A 3-acre outdoor space with water exhibits, whisper dishes, and a musical fence ([Wikipedia).', 'Bubbles: Science in Soap: An interactive exhibit exploring the physics and chemistry of bubbles (Montshire Museum of Science).', 'Nature Trails: Miles of gentle trails through a 100-acre forest bordering the Connecticut River (Montshire Museum of Science).', 'Live Animal Exhibits: Features a hive of honeybees, a colony of leafcutter ants, and local aquatic life (Wikipedia).', 'Play Grove: A whimsical nature-based playscape designed for children to swing, climb, and build (Montshire Museum of Science).', 'Planetary Landscapes: Interactive sculptures that explore dynamic forces and patterns in nature (Montshire Museum of Science).']
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to bring a picnic to enjoy at indoor or outdoor seating areas. For those planning multiple visits, the Montshire Passbook offers discounted admission with no expiration date. Check the museum's event calendar for special workshops and stargazing nights (Montshire Museum of Science) (Wonderful Museums).
attractions.nearby: King Arthur Baking Company (Norwich, VT), Dartmouth College (Hanover, NH), River Roost Brewery (White River Junction, VT) (The Innstead Getaway) (Montshire Museum of Science)

attractions.name: National Life Group Headquarters (National Life Building)
attractions.category: Landmark (Corporate Headquarters / Historic Site) (National Life Group)
attractions.description: The National Life Group headquarters is a prominent landmark in Vermont's capital, serving as the home office for one of the oldest mutual life insurance companies in the U.S. (National Life Group). The current sprawling complex, dedicated in 1960, reflects the company's commitment to staying in Montpelier and features a modernized lobby designed to engage both employees and the community (Arocordis Design). It is known for its architectural significance, environmental LEED certification, and hosting the annual community Do Good Fest on its back lawn (National Life Group).
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday - Sunday: Closed (Yelp)
attractions.admission: Free (The building's public areas like the lobby are free to visit; the annual Do Good Fest has a suggested $10 donation) (Do Good Fest) (Arocordis Design)
highlights: 'Home to one of the first mutual life insurance companies in the U.S., founded in 1848 ([National Life Group)', 'Current headquarters dedicated in 1960, featuring a large campus that remains a major employer in Vermont (National Life Group)', 'LEED-certified building with extensive solar panel arrays contributing to sustainable operations (National Life Group)', "Hosts the annual 'Do Good Fest,' a benefit concert series on the company’s scenic back lawn (Do Good Fest)", 'Features the National Life Trail, a community path connecting the office complex to downtown Montpelier (Vermont Youth Conservation Corps)', 'The lobby previously housed a massive 50-foot historic mural by Paul Sample, which is now on display at the nearby Vermont History Museum (Facebook)']
attractions.tips: Visit the lobby to see the modern renovations and learn about the company's 175-year history (Arocordis Design). Use the National Life Trail for a scenic walk between the campus and downtown Montpelier (Vermont Youth Conservation Corps). If visiting in July, check for the Do Good Fest dates to enjoy live music and food on the lawn (Do Good Fest).
attractions.nearby: Vermont State House, Vermont History Museum, Pavilion Building, Hubbard Park, National Life Trail (Tripadvisor)

Nestled in a gentle bend of the North Branch of the Winooski River, it offers 28 acres of floodplain forest and fields for exploration, education, and stewardship. Source: NBNC About

attractions.description: Positive Pie is an authentic Italian pizzeria that combines old-school heritage with a contemporary Vermont mindset. The restaurant provides a stylish urban refuge where guests can enjoy hand-tossed pizzas, scratch-made eclectic dishes, and carefully crafted beer, wine, and spirit menus in a casual, comfortable atmosphere. (Positive Pie)

Perched on the banks of the Winooski River, it offers a picturesque dining experience in a renovated historic grain warehouse, serving both locals and visitors to the state capital (Sarducci's).

attractions.name: Smugglers' Notch State Park
attractions.description: Smugglers' Notch State Park is a scenic mountain pass through the Green Mountains, featuring 1,000-foot cliffs and a winding road that was historically used for smuggling and the Underground Railroad. The park offers diverse outdoor activities including hiking, bouldering, and ice climbing, with many facilities originally built by the Civilian Conservation Corps (Vermont State Parks, Wikipedia).

attractions.days_open: Daily during the operating season (Vermont State Parks)
attractions.admission: Adult (14+): $5, Child (4-13): $2, Small Child (0-3): FREE (Vermont State Parks)
highlights: 'Hiking on the Long Trail and Sterling Pond Trail ([Wikipedia)', 'Bouldering and ice climbing (Vermont State Parks)', 'Scenic 1,000-foot cliffs and narrow mountain pass (Vermont State Parks)', 'Historic structures built by the Civilian Conservation Corps (Vermont State Parks)', 'Access to Bingham Falls (Vermont State Parks)', 'Cave exploring and biking (Vermont State Parks)']
attractions.tips: The road through the notch (Route 108) is closed during winter. Most campsites are walk-in only, and the narrow road cannot accommodate large RVs. Check-in for camping is at 2:00 PM and check-out is at 11:00 AM (Vermont State Parks).

attractions.name: Winooski River (Onion River)
attractions.description: The Winooski River is a 90-mile-long tributary of Lake Champlain in northern Vermont, significantly forming a major valley through the Green Mountains. It flows southwest through Montpelier, passing along the south side of downtown and the Vermont State House, and is popular for canoeing, kayaking, fishing, and swimming. (Wikipedia)
attractions.address: Stone Cutters Way / Lower State Street, Montpelier, VT 05602
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily (Associated parks typically open dawn to dusk)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: 'Popular for canoeing, kayaking, and fishing for brook, rainbow, and brown trout ([Wikipedia)', 'The Montpelier Recreation Path follows the river for over two miles for walking and biking (Montpelier Alive)', 'Historically significant for timber transportation and hydropower generation (Wikipedia)', 'The river features the Winooski One Dam, once listed as the "world\'s most intelligent dam" (Wikipedia)', 'The confluence with the North Branch River occurs in the heart of downtown Montpelier (City of Montpelier)']
attractions.tips: Access the river for paddling at the Gateway Park boat launch on Lower State Street. For a scenic walk, use the entry points for the Montpelier Bike Path at the high school or Stone Cutters Way. Always check local river gauges for water levels before swimming or paddling. (Montpelier Alive, City of Montpelier)
attractions.nearby: Vermont State House, Hubbard Park, North Branch River Park, Coburn Covered Bridge (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Kismet (Kismet Kitchen)
attractions.description: Kismet is a renowned farm-to-table restaurant in Montpelier, VT, known for its dedication to organic, locally-sourced ingredients and a "cuisine with a conscience" philosophy. Originally opened in 2006, the establishment has been a staple of the local food scene, offering a seasonally inspired menu that accommodates various dietary needs, including vegan and gluten-free options. (Vermont.com, Seven Days)
attractions.hours: Dinner: Wednesday - Saturday 5:00 PM - 9:00 PM; Brunch: Sunday 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM (Montpelier Alive)
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant / No admission fee) (Vermont.com)
highlights: 'Seasonally inspired farm-to-table menu featuring ingredients from local producers like Misty Knoll and Cate Farm ([Seven Days)', 'Extensive non-alcoholic drink menu including fresh-squeezed juices and soothing broths (Seven Days)', 'Renowned Sunday Brunch featuring favorites like Portuguese baked eggs and biscuits and gravy (Seven Days)', 'Intimate and cozy dining atmosphere in its original Barre Street location (Seven Days)', 'Creative craft cocktail program and organic wine selection (Facebook)', 'History of celebrity visitors, including Kevin Bacon and Kyra Sedgwick (Happy Vermont)']
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended for dinner, as the space is intimate with limited seating; walk-ins are accepted for brunch on a first-come, first-served basis. Be sure to try their house-made carrot-cayenne hot sauce, which has received high praise from visitors. (Seven Days, Happy Vermont)
attractions.nearby: Vermont State House, Vermont Historical Society Museum, Hubbard Park, T.W. Wood Gallery (Tripadvisor, Lonely Planet)

attractions.name: Rock of Ages Granite Quarry (E.L. Smith Quarry) Rock of Ages
attractions.category: Landmark / Industrial Site Rock of Ages
attractions.description: The Rock of Ages E.L. Smith Quarry is the world's largest deep-hole monumental granite quarry, reaching depths of nearly 600 feet. It has been a primary source of high-quality Barre Gray granite since its founding in 1885 and stands as a major monument to Vermont's industrial heritage Airial Travel, Atlas Obscura.
attractions.hours: Closed to the public Rock of Ages
attractions.days_open: None (Closed to public) Rock of Ages
attractions.admission: Permanently closed to the public as of January 2025 Rock of Ages
highlights: "World's largest deep-hole dimension granite quarry, measuring 50 acres and nearly 600 feet deep [Airial Travel", 'Source of the famous Barre Gray granite used for memorials and architecture worldwide Village Hiker', 'Features striking turquoise-colored water at the bottom of the quarry pits Rock of Ages Group Tours', 'Served as a filming location for the 2009 Star Trek movie Village Hiker', 'Historic site operated by the Rock of Ages Corporation since the late 19th century Atlas Obscura']
attractions.tips: While the official visitor center and tours are closed, travelers can still view the quarry area via the Millstone Grand Lookout or visit the Vermont Granite Museum for industrial history Rock of Ages, Reddit.
attractions.nearby: Vermont Granite Museum, Hope Cemetery (known for elaborate granite carvings), Millstone Trails Rock of Ages, Hiking Project

attractions.name: Morse Farm Maple Sugarworks
attractions.description: Morse Farm Maple Sugarworks is a historic, family-run farm located just outside of Vermont's capital that has been producing maple syrup for eight generations. Visitors can experience the heritage of Vermont sugarmaking through self-guided tours, multimedia displays in the Woodshed Theatre, and a scenic maple trail winding through the sugarbush. The farm is particularly famous for its maple creemees and local specialty foods available at its well-stocked country store. Morse Farm, Vermont Tourism
attractions.hours: Fall, Winter, & Spring (Labor Day to Memorial Day): 10:00 am – 5:00 pm daily; Summer (Memorial Day to Labor Day): 10:00 am – 7:00 pm daily. Closes at 2:00 pm on Thanksgiving Eve, Christmas Eve, and New Year's Eve. Morse Farm
attractions.days_open: Open daily, year-round (closed on Thanksgiving and Christmas) Morse Farm
attractions.admission: Free (Self-guided tours and syrup tastings are complimentary) Morse Farm, Tripadvisor
highlights: 'Free self-guided sugarhouse tours and syrup tastings [Morse Farm', 'Woodshed Theatre featuring a humorous educational video on sugarmaking Morse Farm', 'Famous maple creemees (Vermont soft-serve) available year-round Morse Farm', 'Maple Trail through the sugarbush with views of the working landscape Morse Farm', 'Farm grounds featuring unique folk art and antique farm implements Tripadvisor', 'Farm animals including goats named Rex and James Morse Farm']
attractions.tips: Be sure to try a maple creemee, which is considered a must-have local treat. Visit during the 'sugaring season' (typically Valentine's Day through mid-April) to see the sugarhouse in active operation with steam rising. The farm is dog-friendly, making it a great stop for travelers with pets. Morse Farm, Two Worlds Treasures
attractions.nearby: Vermont State House, Vermont Historical Society Museum, Hubbard Park and Observation Tower, Bragg Farm Sugarhouse Tripadvisor, Vermont Tourism

attractions.name: Waterbury Village Historic District
attractions.description: Waterbury Village is a historic district in the heart of Vermont's Green Mountains, known for its well-preserved 19th-century architecture and vibrant 'foodie' culture. As a central hub between Montpelier and Burlington, it offers a charming mix of independent shops, local breweries, and historic sites like the 1875 railroad station. Vermont Tourism, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Visitor Center/Cafe: Mon-Fri 7:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Sat-Sun 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Historical Society Museum: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM. Waterbury Historical Society, Discover Waterbury Guide
attractions.days_open: Daily (Specific attractions vary: Visitor Center/Cafe open 7 days; Museum open Mon-Fri). Waterbury Historical Society, Discover Waterbury Guide
attractions.admission: Free (Self-guided walking tours, Rusty Parker Memorial Park, and Waterbury Historical Society Museum are free of charge). Waterbury Historical Society, Discover Waterbury
attractions.phone: 802-560-4298 (Discover Waterbury), 802-244-7033 (Town Office). Discover Waterbury, Waterbury VT Official Site
highlights: ['Historic Waterbury Train Station & Visitor Center', 'Rusty Parker Memorial Park (Concerts & Farmers Markets)', 'Waterbury Historical Society Museum (Dr. Henry Janes House)', 'Prohibition Pig & Local Craft Breweries', 'Stowe Street Shops and Cafes', 'Self-Guided Historic Walking Tours']
attractions.tips: Visit the Train Station first for maps and free coffee samples. Check out the water lines marked on buildings to see the flood history of 1927 and 2011. Discover Waterbury, Go Wandering
attractions.nearby: Ben & Jerry's Factory, Cold Hollow Cider Mill, Waterbury Reservoir, Little River State Park. Vermont Tourism

attractions.name: Ben & Jerry's Factory Tour (The Ben & Jerry's Factory Experience)
attractions.description: The Ben & Jerry's Factory Experience offers a 30-minute guided tour through the brand's first manufacturing site, built in 1985. Visitors enjoy a short film about the company's quirky history and social mission before viewing the production floor from a glassed-in mezzanine. The experience concludes with a delicious ice cream sample in the Flavor Room and access to the whimsical outdoor Flavor Graveyard. Ben & Jerry's Official Site, Tripadvisor
attractions.address: 1281 Waterbury-Stowe Road (Route 100), Waterbury, VT 05676 Ben & Jerry's Official Site
attractions.hours: Monday-Sunday: 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM (Last tour at 5:00 PM) Ben & Jerry's Official Site
attractions.admission: Adults: $6.00, Seniors (65+): $5.00, Children (2-12): $1.00, Infants: Free (plus service fees for online bookings) Ben & Jerry's Factory Tours
highlights: "30-minute guided factory tour including a 'MOO-vie' history presentation [Ben & Jerry's", 'Viewing mezzanine overlooking the production floor manufacturing upwards of 350,000 pints daily The Awkward Traveller', "Ice cream tasting in the Flavor Room with exclusive or classic samples Ben & Jerry's", 'Whimsical outdoor Flavor Graveyard dedicated to retired ice cream flavors Roadside America', "On-site Scoop Shop and Gift Shop for souvenirs and fresh treats Ben & Jerry's", 'Scenic mountain views from the rolling pasture grounds Factory Tours USA']
attractions.tips: Highly recommended to book tickets online 14 days in advance as tours frequently sell out. Plan to arrive early for parking and allow 1 to 1.5 hours for the full visit. Note that active production is not guaranteed on all tour times. The Awkward Traveller, Ben & Jerry's FAQ
attractions.nearby: Cold Hollow Cider Mill, Cabot Farmer's Store, Lake Champlain Chocolates, Smuggler's Notch Distillery, Little River State Park Tripadvisor, New England Wanderlust

attractions.name: Cold Hollow Cider Mill
attractions.description: Housed in a 19th-century farmhouse and historic dairy barn, Cold Hollow Cider Mill is one of Vermont's most iconic year-round destinations. It is world-renowned for its traditional rack-and-cloth cider pressing and its legendary cider donuts, attracting over 300,000 visitors annually. The site features a working cider press, a bakery, a country store, and a hard cider tasting room. (Cold Hollow Cider Mill, Waterbury Roundabout)
attractions.hours: Retail Store: 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM daily. Café: Sun–Thu 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM, Fri–Sat 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM. Breakfast: 8:00 AM – 10:30 AM, Lunch: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM. (Cold Hollow Cider Mill)
attractions.days_open: Open daily year-round (Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year's) (Cold Hollow Cider Mill)
attractions.admission: Free (Cold Hollow Cider Mill FAQs)
highlights: ['Watch fresh cider being pressed on a vintage 1920s rack-and-cloth press.', 'Sample the legendary homemade cider donuts made fresh on-site daily.', 'Visit the live observation beehive to see pollinators in action.', 'Enjoy hard cider tastings and local beer in the Apple Core tasting room.', 'Browse a vast selection of Vermont specialty foods, including cider jelly and maple syrup.', 'Savor a Vermont maple creemee or a full meal at the on-site café.']
attractions.tips: Visit on Mondays or Thursdays between 9:00 AM and 12:00 PM to see the cider press in active operation. Be sure to try a free sample of fresh-pressed cider and don't miss the unique cider jelly, a top seller made by boiling down fresh cider. Peak visitor season occurs during fall foliage from late September through October. (Cold Hollow Cider Mill, Lee Pub Newspapers)
attractions.nearby: Ben & Jerry’s Factory, Cabot Creamery Store, Waterbury Center State Park, Smugglers' Notch Distillery Tasting Room, Vermont Flannel Company, Green Mountain Club. (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: ECHO, Leahy Center for Lake Champlain
attractions.description: ECHO, Leahy Center for Lake Champlain is Vermont's premier science and nature museum, located on the Burlington waterfront overlooking beautiful Lake Champlain. The facility features over 100 interactive exhibits, 70 species of live animal ambassadors, and a 3D science theater, all aimed at inspiring stewardship of the Lake Champlain Basin. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
attractions.admission: Adults: $23 ($20 for VT Residents); Students & Seniors: $21 ($18 for VT Residents); Children (ages 3-17): $20 ($17 for VT Residents); Members: Free; Children 2 and under: Free. 3D Films are an additional $3 for members or $5 for non-members. Official Ticketing
highlights: ['Into the Lake exhibit featuring the largest aquarium tank in Vermont', 'Awesome Forces and Engineer It permanent interactive exhibits', 'Champ Lane, a 2,500-square-foot early learning space for children', 'Over 70 species of fish, reptiles, and amphibians', 'Dealer.com 3D Theater showing science and nature films', 'Dinosaur Safari special exhibit (seasonal)']
attractions.tips: Purchasing tickets online is recommended to avoid lines. Vermont residents should bring a valid ID to receive discounted admission rates. Sensory Friendly Sundays are held on the second Sunday of every month from 9–10 am and are free for visitors with sensory sensitivities. Plan Your Visit
